mnemonics: BIT MAPS HELP (Because It Tracks Most Available Space, Helping Efficiently Locate Pages)
keyPoints: Free space is managed using data structures like bitmaps (bit vectors) and linked lists.
Contiguous allocation can lead to external fragmentation, which is addressed by compaction.
Efficient free space management is critical for overall system performance and preventing file system corruption.
commonMistakes: Confusing external fragmentation (free space is broken up) with internal fragmentation (wasted space within an allocated block).
Assuming that a larger block size is always better; it can lead to increased internal fragmentation.
